original_text:
### Brand Accent Shape (core container device)
- A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**; the other two corners keep a slight radius. Used to house callout text and photography. Reads modern and optimistic.
- Approved radius pairs (print): **1.25" & 0.0625"** (large containers/photos), **0.5" & 0.0625"** (banners), **0.4375" & 0.0625"** (small callout boxes).
- **[GENERAL] Email px conversions (at 96dpi):** large: 120px & 6px; banner: 48px & 6px; callout: 42px & 6px. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right), the 6px radius to the other pair. Be consistent within an email: same diagonal orientation across all sections (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right).
- **Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners** (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

relations.note: Email px conversion is a screen-specific refinement of the print-based accent shape radius pairs.
Charts and tables replace the asymmetric accent-shape radius treatment with a uniform radius.

quotes: A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**
Approved radius pairs (print): **1.25" & 0.0625"** (large containers/photos), **0.5" & 0.0625"** (banners), **0.4375" & 0.0625"** (small callout boxes).
Used to house callout text and photography. Reads modern and optimistic.
Approved radius pairs (print): **1.25" & 0.0625"** (large containers/photos), **0.5" & 0.0625"** (banners), **0.4375" & 0.0625"** (small callout boxes).

rules.intent: Preserve the signature asymmetric-corner brand container device across print applications.
rules.rule_text: The brand's core container device is a rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite-corner radii (the other two corners keep a slight radius) — an asymmetric rounded-corner crop (image.crop.shape) used to house callout text and photography for a modern, optimistic feel. Approved print radius pairs (accent_shape.radius.print, default 1.25in & 0.0625in) are locked: large containers/photos 1.25in & 0.0625in, banners 0.5in & 0.0625in, small callout boxes 0.4375in & 0.0625in.

quotes: same diagonal orientation across all sections (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right)
**[GENERAL] Email px conversions (at 96dpi):** large: 120px & 6px; banner: 48px & 6px; callout: 42px & 6px.
Be consistent within an email: same diagonal orientation across all sections (default: large radius top-left & bottom-right).

rules.intent: Ensure the accent-shape device renders correctly and consistently on screen across an entire email.
rules.rule_text: For email at 96dpi, print accent-shape radius pairs convert to accent_shape.radius.email values (large 120px & 6px, banner 48px & 6px, callout 42px & 6px): apply the large radius to one diagonal corner pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right) and the 6px radius to the other pair. The diagonal orientation (accent_shape.diagonal_orientation, default top-left & bottom-right) must stay consistent across all sections within a single email.

quotes: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners
**Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners** (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

rules.intent: Keep data visualizations legible and neutral by avoiding the decorative asymmetric accent shape.
rules.rule_text: Charts and tables are exempt from the asymmetric brand accent shape; they must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (chart.table.radius = 12px) instead of the enlarged-opposite-corner treatment.
